items.briefing:
With tax time underway, Australians are being urged to use phishing-resistant authentication methods to avoid fraud. Scammers commonly impersonate the ATO during this period, so using passkeys or hardware security keys instead of passwords and SMS codes significantly reduces your risk. Separately, the Five Eyes intelligence alliance has issued a warning about AI being used to enhance cyberattacks, with calls for Australian organisations to accelerate their defences in response.

**Practical tip:** If your myGov or work accounts offer passkeys or an authenticator app, switch to them now — they're much harder for scammers to bypass than SMS codes.

featured.title: Five Eyes AI cyber warning prompts calls for faster defence
featured.quote: AI-powered attacks are outpacing organisational detection capabilities, posing urgent national security risks.
